Oracle Events
Search emails for Raymond Chen's original QC findings using search_emails (query: "QC exceptions" or "Tyler Washington" or "conditions cleared"). Find email_email_76f6a910dc8e from raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files," detailing three exception loans where conditions were marked cleared in the LOS without supporting documentation: LN-2026-00603 (described as Heather Sullivan, closed), LN-2026-00211 (described as open), and LN-2026-00224 (described as open). Email also notes Tyler flagged workload strain in Slack and asked Elena whether people clear conditions before uploading docs. Read full thread using get_thread (thread_id: "email_email_76f6a910dc8e") for complete context including Grace's forwarding to Denise. → Read action.
Search emails for follow-up chain after QC findings using search_emails (query: "processing quality concerns" or "closed file exposure" or "Tyler meeting"). Find: (1) email_email_9e9649a64eac from Grace Yamamoto to Tyler Washington scheduling a 2:30 PM meeting re processing quality concerns; (2) email_email_1855755fe4f4 from Grace Yamamoto to Denise Holloway (CC Raymond Chen), forwarding the QC issue and requesting compliance/investor risk review; (3) email_email_1c8d05bfcc70 from Tyler Washington to Grace Yamamoto, pre-meeting admission acknowledging he cleared conditions without docs, citing workload overload and lock pressure on LN-2026-00603 where Carlos was pushing to keep the file moving; (4) email_email_ccc2b437ae19 from Grace Yamamoto to Denise Holloway and Raymond Chen (CC Carlos Rivera), post-meeting summary confirming Tyler admitted clearing conditions, decision pending on whether this is conduct issue vs training gap vs staffing/process failure, requesting Raymond bring exception detail by file and Denise provide repurchase/compliance risk view. → Read action.
Search Slack for QC-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "QC" or "Tyler" or "conditions" or "cleared"). Find messages in C002 (#loan-processing): (1) message from keystone_e024c13e8759 (Raymond Chen), ts 1774744980, text "Grace, just emailed you the QC summary. 3 Tyler-cleared files w missing support docs, incl LN-2026-00603 which already closed"; (2) message from keystone_e304643b171b (Grace Yamamoto), ts 1774753020, text "Raymond/Denise - I put 9:30 on tomorrow for QC follow-up. Need file-by-file risk + whether this is Tyler only or a bigger processing issue." Also find Tyler's earlier message in C002 about being underwater on conditions and lock files (ts 1774372200). Use conversations_replies for any threaded responses. → Read action.
Search CRM for engagement notes documenting the QC investigation using crm_list_engagements. Find six or more engagement notes including: (1) crm_engagement_221f612776cd, "QC spot check found 3 cleared conds with no PDF support in file. One closed loan may have post-close exposure"; (2) crm_engagement_b10a0d08f620, "QC pulled 3 exception files. LN-2026-00603 already closed with cleared conds lacking file support; Tyler audit trail on all 3"; (3) crm_engagement_285d6fcbe17b, "Ops review set for today after QC findings. Told Tyler to hold file status changes pending meeting"; (4) crm_engagement_9aef5e35b511, "Compliance review requested on closed exception file. Need investor/reg exposure read and whether gap is individual vs process"; (5) crm_engagement_f80c0227687e, "Processor says backlog was heavy and lock pressure on LN-2026-00603 drove rushed clears. Admits docs were not in file when statuses changed"; (6) crm_engagement_09f265e4e5f0, "Post-meeting w Tyler. Admitted conds were cleared before docs uploaded; said workload/lock pressure and informal practice drove it. Decision pending: conduct issue vs training gap vs staffing/process failure." → Read action.
Look up affected loan LN-2026-00603 in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00603") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00603"). Find loan_id los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f with borrower Janet Torres (NOT Heather Sullivan as Raymond's original email stated), status "processing" (NOT "closed" as Raymond reported), loan_amount $260,000, assigned_processor los_staff_266153ea2738 (Rachel Kim, inactive/terminated). Discover borrower name mismatch and status mismatch — these are discrepancies between Raymond's original report and current LOS data that must be flagged. → Read action.
Look up affected loan LN-2026-00211 in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00211") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00211"). Find loan_id los_loan_223826af9a5d with borrower Elizabeth Gray, status "withdrawn" (NOT "open" as Raymond reported), loan_amount $217,000, assigned_processor los_staff_afc9caafae9d (Sofia Reyes). Discover status mismatch — Raymond's email said "open" but LOS shows "withdrawn." → Read action.
Look up affected loan LN-2026-00224 in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00224") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00224"). Find loan_id los_loan_48275b0214f9 with borrower Steven Thomas, status "closed" with closing_date 2026-03-10 (NOT "open" as Raymond reported), loan_amount $184,000, assigned_processor los_staff_c4dab6a67e8f (Andre Washington, inactive/terminated). Discover status mismatch — Raymond's email said "open" but LOS shows "closed." → Read action.
Search emails and Slack for Tyler Washington's involvement on other recent files using search_emails (query: "Tyler Washington" or "LN-2026-00556") and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Tyler" or "LN-2026-00556" or "LN-2026-00611"). Find: (1) LN-2026-00556 — Tyler submitted to UWM without running the automated compliance check, UWM kicked the file back with 15 underwriting conditions (email_email_c156abc48685 from Tyler to casey.morgan@uwm.com, email_email_469925ec064e from UWM returning the file). Elena Marchetti coached Tyler on the pre-sub checklist (email_email subject "Pre-sub steps for LN-2026-00556"). Tyler's own Slack message in C002 (ts 1774380780): "UWM kicked back LN-2026-00556 w/ 15 conds. A bunch look like stuff I should've caught pre-sub." (2) LN-2026-00611 — Tyler assisting on a day-75 file with expired documents, posted Slack message in C002 (ts 1774372920) about putting follow-ups on his calendar for Baker LN-2026-00611. These files show a pattern of Tyler skipping process steps under workload pressure. → Read action.
Look up Denise Holloway's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise Holloway"). Find denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com (contact_id: staff_a989261d4d33, Compliance Officer, Keystone Mortgage Partners). Needed to send the formal summary email. → Read action.
Add QC follow-up note to loan LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f", action: "QC follow-up" or similar, detail: summary of current remediation status including that conditions were cleared without supporting documentation, Tyler admitted to the issue, post-meeting determination is still pending between conduct issue / training gap / staffing failure, and noting the discrepancy between original QC report and current LOS data, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com"). → Write action.
Add QC follow-up note to loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action: "QC follow-up" or similar, detail: summary of remediation status, noting conditions cleared without docs by Tyler Washington, current LOS status is "withdrawn" which differs from original report, determination still pending, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com"). → Write action.
Add QC follow-up note to loan LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9", action: "QC follow-up" or similar, detail: summary of remediation status, noting conditions cleared without docs by Tyler Washington, current LOS status is "closed" which differs from original report of "open", determination still pending, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com"). → Write action.
Send formal summary email to Denise Holloway using send_email (sender: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: "QC Follow-Up Summary — Tyler Washington Condition-Clearing Exceptions" or similar, content covering: (1) LN-2026-00603 with borrower name discrepancy and status discrepancy flagged; (2) LN-2026-00211 with status discrepancy (open vs withdrawn); (3) LN-2026-00224 with status discrepancy (open vs closed); (4) Tyler's admission and the pending determination between conduct/training/process failure; (5) Tyler's pattern on other files (LN-2026-00556 with 15 UWM conditions, LN-2026-00611 day-75 file); (6) what remains unresolved so Denise can close her compliance review). → Write action.
Post update in #compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004", payload: summary of QC follow-up status covering the Tyler Washington condition-clearing findings across LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00224, remediation progress, outstanding items including pending corrective action determination, and data discrepancies discovered during loan verification). → Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
